target.title: feat(hooks): add agent and tool lifecycle boundaries
target.body:
## Why Agent execution lacked complete lifecycle boundaries around thinking, response generation, and tool execution, reducing traceability for observability and policy systems. This PR adds those boundaries and ensures tool lifecycle hooks carry `toolCallId` context when available. lobster-biscuit ## Split Context This PR was accidentally closed after a messed up rebase https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/12583 which was originally split from closed umbrella PR #9761: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/9761. ## Detailed Changes - Added internal agent lifecycle hook emissions: - `agent:thinking:start` / `agent:thinking:end` - `agent:response:start` / `agent:response:end` - `agent:tool:start` / `agent:tool:end` - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for tool execution lifecycle completion - Propagated `toolCallId` through before/after tool hook contexts where available - Extended client tool definition adapter to pass `toolCallId` and run after-tool hook flow - Added focused tests for agent lifecycle emissions and tool lifecycle behavior ## Related Links, Issues and Resolution - Closed source PR: #12583 AND #9761 - Related issues: - Closes #7724 - Closes #7597 - Closes #5513 - #7985 - #6095 - #6264 - #6885

Greptile Summary

This PR adds complete lifecycle boundaries for agent execution (thinking, response generation, and tool execution), improving observability and traceability for policy systems. The implementation propagates `toolCallId` through tool lifecycle hooks and ensures consistent hook firing across different execution paths. Key improvements: - Split lifecycle hook logic into dedicated `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module - Ensured `agent:thinking:start/end` fire for all agent runs - Added conditional `agent:response:start/end` hooks that only fire when response output is generated - Propagated `toolCallId` to `before_tool_call` and `after_tool_call` hooks - Added `runAfterToolCallHook` wiring for both success and error paths in tool execution - Comprehensive test coverage validates hook emissions in different scenarios

Confidence Score: 5/5

- Safe to merge - well-structured refactoring with comprehensive test coverage - The changes are well-architected with proper error handling throughout. The new `lifecycle-hooks.ts` module cleanly separates concerns, all hook emission paths include error handling that logs warnings without failing execution, and the test coverage validates the core lifecycle hook behavior. The `toolCallId` propagation is handled consistently with proper fallbacks. - No files require special attention Last reviewed commit: d48824c

target.title: fix(update): hand off Linux service auto-updates
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91823.

Linux gateway services installed with the older generic OpenClaw service markers can miss systemd-specific env hints during startup auto-update. That made startup auto-update run `openclaw update` as a direct child of the live gateway process, where the update command correctly refuses to mutate the active gateway service process.

This keeps Linux marker-only supervisor detection opt-in and applies it only to update-specific paths:

- startup auto-update now routes marker-only Linux OpenClaw gateway services through managed systemd handoff
- update respawn now uses the same opt-in classification so the post-update restart path stays consistent
- generic restart and default supervisor detection continue to ignore marker-only Linux service env

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/supervisor-markers.test.ts src/infra/update-startup.test.ts src/infra/process-respawn.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first run found a valid consistency issue between startup handoff and update respawn
  - final run: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Linux startup auto-update failed under a real user systemd gateway service when the service env had only stale generic OpenClaw service markers and no systemd-specific hints.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox, real user systemd service, packed OpenClaw package installed into a temporary npm prefix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built and packed the branch, installed the tarball, created `openclaw-gateway.service` with `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw`, `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=gateway`, and `UnsetEnvironment=OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT INVOCATION_ID SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID JOURNAL_STREAM`, then started the gateway with auto-update enabled.

Evidence after fix: Testbox-through-Crabbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktwdrz6dq71amtvgk027qxch`, Actions run `27382176713`; output included `patch_present=1`, `health={"ok":true,"status":"live"}`, and `auto-update handoff started`.

Observed result after fix: The stale marker-only Linux service started managed update handoff and did not log `auto-update attempt failed`.

What was not tested: A full package update to completion was not allowed to mutate a persistent user install; the proof verifies the broken startup decision and handoff routing in an isolated packed-install service.

## Regression repro

Unpatched `origin/main` was rebuilt and tested with the same stale service shape.

- Testbox-through-Crabbox provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ktwec4jjmhaf9em2xp2w1tr6`, Actions run `27382620484`
- output included `patch_present=0`, `health={"ok":true,"status":"live"}`, `auto-update attempt failed`, and manual `openclaw update` exited `1` with `Package updates cannot run from inside the gateway service process.`

target.title: fix(codex): return diagnostic snapshot on app inventory refresh failure
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes sub-problem #2 of #91352: native Codex `codexPlugins` app inventory refresh failures no longer propagate as unhandled rejections, so normal Codex turns can continue without plugin apps instead of failing the harness.

When `app/list` fails in `CodexAppInventoryCache.refreshUncoalesced`, the method now returns a best-effort `CodexAppInventorySnapshot` carrying a `lastError` diagnostic. Callers that already try/catch `refreshNow` (thread config and plugin activation) continue to work, and the cache no longer forces every consumer to handle rejection propagation.

## Changes

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts`
  - Replace `throw error` in `refreshUncoalesced` with a diagnostic snapshot return.
  - If a previous successful snapshot exists, preserve its apps and attach `lastError`.
  - If no prior snapshot exists, return an empty snapshot with `expiresAtMs: 0` so `read()` still reports `missing` on the next lookup.

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts`
  - Update existing test to assert resolve-with-diagnostic instead of rejection.
  - Add regression test for the first-refresh-failure case (empty cache, no prior entry).

## Verification

```bash
# Focused cache tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# 8 passed

# Thread config tests that exercise refreshNow through buildCodexPluginThreadConfig
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-thread-config.test.ts
# 14 passed

# Plugin inventory tests
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/plugin-inventory.test.ts
# 5 passed

# Lint
npx oxlint --import-plugin --config .oxlintrc.json   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.ts   extensions/codex/src/app-server/app-inventory-cache.test.ts
# clean
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Codex `app/list` refresh failures used to throw from `CodexAppInventoryCache.refreshNow`, which could fail the harness turn. After the patch, the cache returns a diagnostic snapshot so the turn continues without plugin apps.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout at commit `72b5ab5d76` on Node `$(node --version)`, running the actual `CodexAppInventoryCache` code (not a unit-test wrapper).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
pnpm exec tsx scripts/repro/91359-codex-app-list-failure-proof.mts
```

Script content: `scripts/repro/91359-codex-app-list-failure-proof.mts`

**Evidence after fix:**

```
=== PR #91359 Codex app/list failure proof ===

refreshNow returned (did not throw): true
snapshot.lastError: simulated app/list failure
snapshot.apps: []
snapshot.expiresAtMs: 0
cache.read state: missing
cache.read diagnostic: simulated app/list failure

PASS: failure handled gracefully.
```

**Observed result after fix:** When `refreshNow` is called with a request that throws, it returns a snapshot containing `lastError`, `apps: []`, and `expiresAtMs: 0` instead of rejecting. A subsequent `cache.read` reports `state: "missing"` and surfaces the diagnostic. This matches the behavior the harness relies on to continue a turn without plugin apps.

**What was not tested:** A live end-to-end Codex harness run against a real failing app server; this proof exercises the exact cache code path that the harness uses during turn setup.

Closes #91352 (sub-problem #2)

target.title: fix(update): preserve local package overrides
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserves local package overrides during package update flows, including standalone added `dist` files, and reports the recovery bundle when overrides are not reapplied.
- Keeps content-inventory validation strict for new tarballs while allowing already-published `2026.6.5` / `2026.6.5-beta.6` packages that predate `dist/postinstall-content-inventory.json`.
- Repairs the `main` merge conflict on `src/cli/update-cli.ts` and `src/infra/update-runner.ts`, then refreshes the branch to current `upstream/main`.
- Adds the missing zh-CN glossary entries needed for the docs check after the latest main merge.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm check:docs`
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/package-local-overrides.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts docs/.i18n/glossary.zh-CN.json`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/check-openclaw-package-tarball.test.ts test/scripts/postinstall-bundled-plugins.test.ts test/scripts/openclaw-cross-os-release-checks.test.ts src/infra/package-dist-inventory.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/infra/update-runner.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/progress.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH TMPDIR=/tmp node --import tsx --input-type=module` current-head local package override smoke
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` clean after the accepted standalone-added-file finding was fixed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Package self-update preserves locally modified installed package files by default, captures standalone added `dist` files in the recovery bundle, reapplies trusted overrides only when requested, reports conflict file paths in human output when upstream changed the same file, and still exposes the preserved recovery bundle when package verification fails after a partial install mutation. This refresh also hardens local override path validation, rollback error reporting, package content-inventory enforcement, package-root tarball inventory validation, and release candidate symlink checks so they apply to files npm would actually package.

Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout on current PR head `2ae1833519adf9b211a84454461fad2d7a9647da`, Node `v24.14.0`. The current-head smoke used real filesystem temp package roots and the real `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps` / `writePackageDistInventory` implementation through `node --import tsx`; it was not a Vitest/mock assertion path. Focused Vitest coverage also exercised the tarball checker, cross-OS release inventory generation, update progress rendering, package inventory verification, package update runner, and update CLI behavior.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a current-head terminal smoke with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`, creating temporary installed package roots, writing real package dist inventories, mutating installed `dist/index.js` as a local override, applying update contents, invoking `runGlobalPackageUpdateSteps`, and rendering `printResult` for a conflict result. Then ran focused tests, formatter check, oxlint, `git diff --check`, and nested autoreview on the same head.

Evidence after fix:

```text
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_CURRENT_HEAD_SMOKE default-preserve {"failedStep":null,"failedTail":null,"status":"preserved","conflicts":[],"installed":"export const updated = true;\n","recovery":"export const local = true;\n","steps":["global update"]}
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_CURRENT_HEAD_SMOKE missing-content-inventory-reject {"failedStep":"global install verify","failedTail":"Invalid package dist content inventory at dist/postinstall-content-inventory.json: expected packaged file hashes to match current dist files.","status":"preserved","conflicts":[],"installed":"export const updated_without_inventory = true;\n","recovery":"export const local = true;\n","steps":["global update"]}

Update Result: OK
  Local changes: conflict (0 added, 1 modified, 0 deleted)
  Local recovery: /tmp/openclaw-local-overrides-proof
  Local conflict: dist/index.js (target-changed)

Total time: 1ms
OPENCLAW_UPDATE_CURRENT_HEAD_SMOKE conflict-output-printed
```

Supplemental current-head proof:

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  157 passed (157)

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  109 passed (109)

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  153 passed (153)
```

```text
All matched files use the correct format.
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ... passed.
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check passed.
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.78)
```

Observed result after fix: Default update preserved the local override, left the updated installed file in place, and stored the original local file in the recovery bundle. Package verification rejected the partially mutated package when content inventory no longer matched the installed `dist` contents, while still preserving the local override recovery bundle. Human non-JSON update output now includes the conflict path and reason: `dist/index.js (target-changed)`. The focused tests additionally proved top-level tarball files cannot satisfy package-scoped content inventory requirements, npm-omitted symlinks no longer block release candidate inventory generation, and the optional-dependency fallback fixture writes a valid post-update package inventory before verification.

What was not tested: A full `pnpm check` / full repository Vitest run was not rerun after the latest autoreview fixes because the touched surface was covered by the focused update, tarball, postinstall, cross-OS release, inventory, runner, CLI option/progress tests, formatter, oxlint, diff check, current-head real filesystem smoke, and nested autoreview. A fresh remote Crabbox run was not repeated after this local fix; the current-head real smoke above is the after-fix behavior proof for this branch.

target.title: fix(openai): omit gpt-5.5 tool reasoning effort
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes Azure/OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions tool payloads for `gpt-5.5` by omitting `reasoning_effort` when tools are present.
- Extends the existing `gpt-5.4-mini` safeguard to the `gpt-5.5` family, matching the upstream rejection observed from Azure OpenAI.
- Adds regression coverage so `gpt-5.5` tool payloads keep tools but do not include `reasoning_effort`.

## Linked context
Which issue does this close?
Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?
Related #76176

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
No. This was found while validating `azure-openai/gpt-5.5` in a real OpenClaw gateway setup.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway agent run using `azure-openai/gpt-5.5` failed when OpenClaw sent Chat Completions tool payloads with `reasoning_effort`.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw 2026.6.1 gateway, Azure OpenAI-compatible `/openai/v1` endpoint, `azure-openai/gpt-5.5` configured as the default model.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Validated the local runtime mitigation by setting `compat.supportsReasoningEffort: false` for `azure-openai/gpt-5.5`, restarting the gateway, then running a Gateway agent turn with `azure-openai/gpt-5.5`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  ```json
  {
    "executionTrace": {
      "winnerProvider": "azure-openai",
      "winnerModel": "gpt-5.5",
      "attempts": [
        {
          "provider": "azure-openai",
          "model": "gpt-5.5",
          "result": "success",
          "stage": "assistant"
        }
      ],
      "fallbackUsed": false,
      "runner": "embedded"
    },
    "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK_AGENT_55"
  }
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The agent turn completed successfully on `azure-openai/gpt-5.5` without fallback.
- What was not tested: I did not run a locally built OpenClaw binary from this branch against the live Azure endpoint; the live proof used the equivalent runtime compatibility mitigation in the installed gateway, and this PR adds source-level behavior plus regression coverage.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Azure resource names, paths, account identifiers, and secrets are intentionally omitted/redacted.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  ```text
  400 Function tools with reasoning_effort are not supported for gpt-5.5 in /v1/chat/completions. Please use /v1/responses instead.
  ```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```text
.\\node_modules\\.bin\\vitest.cmd run src\\agents\\openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "reasoning_effort"
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added coverage that `gpt-5.5` Chat Completions payloads with tools omit `reasoning_effort` while preserving the tool payload.

What failed before this fix, if known?

A real Gateway agent run failed with Azure rejecting `Function tools with reasoning_effort` for `gpt-5.5` on `/v1/chat/completions`.

If no test was added, why not?

A regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
Yes. `gpt-5.5` Chat Completions tool calls no longer request `reasoning_effort`.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)
No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The highest-risk area is changing request shaping for `gpt-5.5` Chat Completions when tools are present.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is scoped to tool payloads for the `gpt-5.5` family and mirrors the existing `gpt-5.4-mini` safeguard. Non-tool payloads still keep normal reasoning-effort behavior.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on maintainer review and CI.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet.

## Real behavior proof

After applying the local GPT-5.5 compatibility change in a real OpenClaw setup, the installed OpenClaw gateway can call the Azure GPT-5.5 deployment directly without falling back:

```console
$ ~/.npm-global/bin/openclaw infer model run --gateway --model azure-openai/gpt-5.5 --prompt "Reply with exactly OC_GPT55_OK." --json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "gateway",
  "provider": "azure-openai",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "text": "OC_GPT55_OK",
      "mediaUrl": null
    }
  ]
}
```

I also verified the changed request builder locally against the source checkout: Azure GPT-5.5 deployment aliases with tools omit `reasoning_effort`, while custom providers with explicit reasoning compat and Azure no-tools payloads keep it.


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Azure GPT-5.5 can be used through the OpenClaw gateway without falling back, and the code change prevents the failing Azure Chat Completions + tools + `reasoning_effort` request shape.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw runtime on WSL Ubuntu 24.04, installed CLI at `~/.npm-global/bin/openclaw`, connected to the configured Azure OpenAI GPT-5.5 deployment through the local OpenClaw gateway.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```console
$ cd ~/.openclaw
$ ~/.npm-global/bin/openclaw infer model run --gateway --model azure-openai/gpt-5.5 --prompt "Reply with exactly OC_GPT55_OK." --json
```

**Evidence after fix:** Live console output from the real OpenClaw gateway run:
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "capability": "model.run",
  "transport": "gateway",
  "provider": "azure-openai",
  "model": "gpt-5.5",
  "attempts": [],
  "outputs": [
    {
      "text": "OC_GPT55_OK",
      "mediaUrl": null
    }
  ]
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The request completed successfully with `provider: azure-openai`, `model: gpt-5.5`, no fallback attempts, and the expected `OC_GPT55_OK` output.

**What was not tested:** No additional gaps.

target.title: Polish Workboard operations view
target.body:
## Summary
- polish Workboard card layout, timestamps, density controls, detail drawer behavior, mobile modal/dropdown behavior, and tooltip handling
- add health summary badge highlighting, filter-aware views, stable card action slots, card-derived agent filter options, and better empty states
- add guarded Workboard auto-refresh with dispatch-safe refresh sequencing, stale refresh protection, and disconnect cleanup
- fix Workboard lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes

## Change breakdown

### Workboard controls and refresh behavior
- Stabilized the toolbar so refresh state, auto-refresh, dispatch, density toggle, archive toggle, and last-updated time do not jump around during refreshes.
- Made the last-updated display a stable timestamp instead of switching between transient refresh text and time text.
- Hid the manual Refresh button while auto-refresh is enabled to reduce repeated visual state changes.
- Kept Dispatch ready work available during refresh when the action itself is still valid.
- Added guarded refresh sequencing so stale refresh responses do not overwrite newer Workboard state.

### Card layout and details
- Added per-card updated timestamps with local browser-time rendering.
- Reworked card headers so actions, timestamps, and chips use stable positions across density modes.
- Made card action slots consistent so buttons do not jump as density or content changes.
- Changed compact timestamp copy to use the clock icon plus date/time instead of truncating `Updated: ...`.
- Updated the details drawer to show matching date and time precision.
- Made clicking the already-selected card close the details drawer.

### Health metrics and highlighting
- Added the Workboard health metric bar for running, blocked, stale, ready unassigned, missing proof, and failed attempts.
- Made metric badges interactive: clicking a metric highlights matching cards.
- Matched highlight colors to the selected metric instead of always using red.
- Highlighted the active metric badge so the current metric selection is visible.
- Blended selected-card side color into the highlighted border so the card treatment looks intentional.
- Made health counts derive from live card/task/session state instead of manual labels.

### Filtering and dropdowns
- Replaced inconsistent native/custom filter dropdown behavior with a shared Workboard select control.
- Made selecting one dropdown close the previously open dropdown.
- Capitalized priority labels for consistency.
- Disabled zero-result view preset options where appropriate and added an empty-state message for no-result filters.
- Made status/view filters hide zero-result columns so filtered views are useful.
- Limited single-column filtered layouts on desktop instead of stretching one column across the full screen.
- Improved agent filter/assignment options by deduping configured agents, default/unassigned agent handling, and card-assignee-derived agents.

### Tooltips and icons
- Replaced unclear density icons with clearer list/dense-layout icons.
- Added tooltips across Workboard icon buttons.
- Reduced tooltip delay globally for Workboard buttons.
- Restored the compact one-line tooltip style and fixed tooltip clipping/overlap issues.
- Suppressed hover tooltips on mobile detail interactions where they obscure the fullscreen panel.

### Mobile and responsive behavior
- Fixed mobile horizontal overflow so cards fit within the board container without x-scroll.
- Restored card visibility after the mobile column/layout adjustment.
- Made the new-card modal fullscreen on mobile and larger on desktop.
- Fixed mobile dropdown placement inside the new-card modal.
- Changed modal dropdown menus to overlay instead of consuming layout real estate.
- Made mobile details open as a fullscreen panel.

### New card and demo data flows
- Improved the new-card modal layout to avoid nested vertical scroll problems.
- Added Workboard CLI/demo proof data for live boards, including seeded cards across statuses except Running.
- Added and duplicated demo card batches for the requested agents/boards during validation.

### Tests, i18n, and CI
- Added/updated Workboard controller, view, tooltip, lifecycle, settings, and E2E coverage.
- Regenerated Control UI i18n outputs for the new Workboard strings.
- Fixed CI lint issues from the custom dropdown and health-filter changes.
- Updated PR proof with live Workboard CLI output so the real-behavior proof gate passes.

## Verification
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts` - 79 tests passed, including the regression for clearing stale task summaries after dispatch task refresh failure.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts` - 5 files, 184 tests passed.
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm ui:i18n:check`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH pnpm tsgo:test:ui`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high` - first run found the stale task-summary dispatch issue; after commit `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, rerun was clean with no accepted/actionable findings.
- Current branch proof was run at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df` against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Workboard operations view layout, filters, health badges, tooltip-safe card actions, custom dropdowns, edit/create modals, drag/reload flow, read-only behavior, guarded auto-refresh, diagnostics refresh on writable tab load, stale-base CI failures, and stale task-summary cleanup when dispatch relists cards but task enrichment fails.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR branch at head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`, reviewed and diff-checked against current `upstream/main` `9827490f5f735a567716f128ffcdb892d9d39324`, using the real local Workboard plugin state plus focused deterministic Workboard/UI tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```sh
pnpm openclaw --version
pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/workboard.test.ts ui/src/ui/dom-tooltips.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle.node.test.ts
pnpm ui:i18n:check
pnpm tsgo:test:ui
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/workboard.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI on current head `b89813c`:
```text
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (b89813c)

$ pnpm openclaw plugins list --enabled --json | node -e '<summarize enabled Workboard plugin>'
{
  "enabledCount": 80,
  "workboard": {
    "id": "workboard",
    "enabled": true
  }
}

$ pnpm openclaw workboard list --json | node -e '<summarize live card statuses without printing card contents>'
{
  "cardCount": 10,
  "statuses": {
    "triage": 1,
    "backlog": 1,
    "todo": 1,
    "scheduled": 1,
    "ready": 3,
    "review": 1,
    "blocked": 1,
    "done": 1
  },
  "readyByAgent": {
    "main": 2,
    "gadget": 1
  }
}
```

Linked visual proof from the Chromium Workboard E2E artifact set:
- Create dialog screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/3oczzf.png
- Edited card screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/4b90q2.png
- Lifecycle review screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/anspcb.png
- Read-only board screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/21bnki.png
- Drag/status persistence screenshot: https://files.catbox.moe/ggu4pd.png
- Writable flow recording: https://files.catbox.moe/1asjze.webm

Observed result after fix: The live Workboard plugin is enabled and the real Workboard CLI lists cards across expected non-running statuses, including three Ready cards for dispatch/view validation. The dispatch controller now clears cached task summaries before attempting post-dispatch task enrichment, so a task-ledger failure can record `lastRefreshError` without reusing stale task state against the freshly listed cards. The local CLI also reported a config-version mismatch and plugin index migration warnings for the existing local state; those warnings did not block Workboard plugin discovery or card listing. Focused Workboard tests, UI typecheck, i18n check, formatting check, diff whitespace check, and branch autoreview all pass on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.

What was not tested: A live production Gateway with private user data was not dumped in the PR; live proof summarizes plugin/card state without printing card contents or secrets. Full CI is rerunning on pushed head `b89813ccc6f6c8517160d65d7ec30bc242cf17df`.

target.title: feat(gateway): startup watchdog dumps in-flight phases on hang
target.body:
## Summary

Add a one-shot startup watchdog to the gateway CLI that dumps the **in-flight** diagnostic phase stack to `stderr` as a single structured line when the HTTP server has not bound within a configurable threshold (default 60s). Diagnostic-only — no behavior change on the happy path.

## Why

When the gateway hangs anywhere between `[gateway] starting...` and HTTP listen-success, the only externally visible signal today is "no log output for N minutes until the orchestrator SIGKILLs the pod." Every intervening step already runs inside `withDiagnosticPhase(name, run)` blocks, but those snapshots are only emitted on phase **completion** via the optional `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE` logger — so on the hang path nothing is ever flushed. Operators end up having to attach a debugger or guess.

This change surfaces *which* startup step is stuck, with a per-phase elapsed timer, without altering any production code path on the success path.

## Source-line navigation (upstream `a1f1895b1b` base)

For reviewers navigating the diff context against the PR base:

| Anchor | File:line on base |
| --- | --- |
| `gatewayLog.info("starting...")` log emit | `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts:815` |
| `startupTrace.mark("cli.gateway-loop")` (ARM-site anchor) | `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts:816` |
| `activePhaseStack` (module-level mutable, snapshot source) | `src/logging/diagnostic-phase.ts:21` |
| `withDiagnosticPhase` (push/pop owner of `activePhaseStack`) | `src/logging/diagnostic-phase.ts:71` |
| `onListening` (HTTP-listen-success, CANCEL-site anchor) | `src/gateway/server/http-listen.ts:36` |

## What changes

### `src/logging/diagnostic-phase.ts`
- Adds `getActiveDiagnosticPhases(): ActiveDiagnosticPhaseSnapshot[]`. Returns a **deep-copy** snapshot of the currently in-flight phase stack, ordered outermost → innermost, with `elapsedMs` computed from `performance.now()` at snapshot time. The returned array/objects are fresh — mutating them does not affect future phase tracking. Safe to call from a synchronous timer callback on a starved event loop (one stack walk + N `performance.now()` reads).

### `src/logging/gateway-startup-watchdog.ts` (new)
- `armStartupWatchdog({ thresholdMs })` — arms a single `setTimeout`. Idempotent (a second call while armed is a no-op). The handle is `.unref()`'d so the diagnostic timer never keeps the process alive on its own.
- `cancelStartupWatchdog()` — clears the timer. Safe to call multiple times and safe to call when nothing is armed.
- `resolveStartupWatchdogThresholdMs(env)` — reads `OPENCLAW_STARTUP_WATCHDOG_MS`. Defaults to `60_000`. `0` disables the watchdog. **Malformed overrides (negative, non-numeric, `NaN`, `Infinity`, `-Infinity`) return `0` (disabled) — they do NOT silently re-arm the default.** Defensive parsing for an external-boundary value: a bad operator override should be visible (no spurious watchdog lines) rather than masked by the default.
- On fire: snapshots `getActiveDiagnosticPhases()`, formats a single line, writes it **directly to `process.stderr`** (bypassing the logger so a starved or filtered logging pipeline can't eat the diagnostic on the hang path), and exits. Never re-arms, never retries, never throws.
- **Phase names in the rendered line are JSON-quoted in both `stuck step=...` and inside `pending=[...]`**, so an embedded `"` or `\n` cannot break the single-line parse contract operators rely on when grepping production stderr.

Example output:

```text
[startup-watchdog] stuck step="server.import-start" elapsed=1501.0ms threshold=1500ms pending=["cli.config-snapshot"@1501.1ms "server.import-start"@1501.0ms]
```

### `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts`
- Calls `armStartupWatchdog({ thresholdMs: resolveStartupWatchdogThresholdMs() })` right after the existing `startupTrace.mark("cli.gateway-loop")` (base anchor `run.ts:816`), immediately after the `[gateway] starting...` log emit at base anchor `run.ts:815`.
- Calls `cancelStartupWatchdog()` at the top of the startup failure `catch` block so the watchdog cannot interleave a "stuck step" line on top of a real error message already being reported.

### `src/gateway/server/http-listen.ts`
- Calls `cancelStartupWatchdog()` inside `onListening` (base anchor `http-listen.ts:36`, immediately before `resolve()`), so listen-success is the primary cancel point. The call is idempotent and safe when no watchdog is armed (every caller other than the gateway CLI never arms one).

## Tests

- `src/logging/gateway-startup-watchdog.test.ts` — 17 tests covering:
  - Env-var threshold parsing (default, blank, `0` disable, positive override).
  - **`NaN` / negative / non-numeric / `Infinity` / `-Infinity` overrides → disabled (return `0`), NOT default** (defensive parsing for external-boundary input).
  - Arm/no-arm semantics (threshold 0, NaN, negative, double-arm refusal).
  - Single stderr line on fire, with the in-flight 2-level phase tree captured via real `withDiagnosticPhase` wrappers and `vi.useFakeTimers()`. Asserts the `pending=[...]` rendering uses JSON-quoted phase names.
  - Cancellation before threshold prevents emit.
  - Fire-at-most-once even past the threshold.
  - Empty-stack rendering as `pending=[]`.
  - Stderr-write-failure tolerated without crashing startup.
  - **Embedded-newline + embedded-quote in phase name** is escaped so exactly one trailing newline is emitted and both the `stuck step=` field and the `pending=[...]` entry use JSON-escaped output.
- `src/logging/diagnostic-phase.test.ts` — 5 tests covering `getActiveDiagnosticPhases()`:
  - Returns `[]` when no phase has ever been active.
  - **Returns `[]` AFTER all phases have exited (stale-stack edge)** — belt-and-suspenders so a watchdog firing outside any phase can never see a leftover frame whose `elapsedMs` would be computed against a stale `startedWallMs`.
  - **Returns `[]` even when the phase finished with a thrown error** (finally-cleanup guarantee).
  - Snapshots outermost → innermost with `elapsedMs` populated.
  - Deep-copy guarantee (mutating the snapshot does not affect subsequent snapshots).
- `src/gateway/server/http-listen.test.ts` — re-run, all 3 existing tests still pass with the cancel call in `onListening`.

24/24 logging + diagnostic-phase tests pass on this PR's HEAD; existing logging + http-listen tests unaffected. `pnpm tsgo:core:test` clean. `pnpm lint` produces no new errors on the touched files (pre-existing main-branch lint failures in `update-cli`, `extensions/`, `scripts/` are unchanged).

## Configuration

| Env var | Default | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `OPENCLAW_STARTUP_WATCHDOG_MS` | `60000` | Milliseconds before the watchdog fires. Set to `0` to disable entirely. Malformed values (negative, non-numeric, `NaN`, `±Infinity`) also disable the watchdog rather than silently falling back to the default. |

## Non-goals / out of scope

- No change to HTTP/metrics surfaces.
- No re-arm or retry logic. The watchdog observes once and logs once.
- No async I/O in the timer callback (no `await`, no promises, no `gatewayLog.*`) — the whole point is to surface diagnostic state when the logger or event loop is starved.

## Layering note

`gateway-startup-watchdog.ts` lives in `src/logging/` (next to `diagnostic-phase.ts`) rather than under `src/cli/gateway-cli/` so the HTTP-listen helper can `import` the cancel hook without adding a `src/gateway/server/ → src/cli/` dependency edge. The watchdog is a singleton owned by whoever arms it (today: the gateway CLI startup loop; nobody else arms one).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Gateway hang anywhere between the `[gateway] starting...` log emit and HTTP listen-success leaves operators with no externally-visible signal until the orchestrator `SIGKILL`s the pod. This patch surfaces a single structured stderr line naming the stuck phase + its in-flight ancestor stack + per-phase elapsed time when the bind has not happened within `OPENCLAW_STARTUP_WATCHDOG_MS` (default 60000 ms). Tracking: smisy/daily-ai#364 (GH-5814 class).

**Real environment tested:** Local `darwin/arm64` Mac Studio, Node v25.7.0, the production `withDiagnosticPhase` + `diagnostic-phase` + `gateway-startup-watchdog` modules built into this PR. No mock substitution: the live `withDiagnosticPhase` from `src/logging/diagnostic-phase.ts` is the source of the in-flight stack, the live `armStartupWatchdog` from `src/logging/gateway-startup-watchdog.ts` schedules a real `setTimeout`, and the real `process.stderr.write` carries the diagnostic line out of process. Same modules, same code path the gateway uses in production, fired end-to-end.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From a worktree at this PR's HEAD `dc1972766a`:

```bash
cat > smoke-watchdog.ts <<'EOF'
import {
  withDiagnosticPhase, resetDiagnosticPhasesForTest,
} from "./src/logging/diagnostic-phase.ts";
import {
  armStartupWatchdog, resetStartupWatchdogForTest,
} from "./src/logging/gateway-startup-watchdog.ts";

resetDiagnosticPhasesForTest();
resetStartupWatchdogForTest();

const origWrite = process.stderr.write.bind(process.stderr);
process.stderr.write = ((chunk: string | Uint8Array): boolean => {
  const s = typeof chunk === "string" ? chunk : chunk.toString();
  process.stdout.write("STDERR-CAPTURE> " + s);
  return true;
}) as typeof process.stderr.write;

armStartupWatchdog({ thresholdMs: 1500 });

let resolveInner: (() => void) | undefined;
const innerPending = new Promise<void>((res) => { resolveInner = res; });

const outer = withDiagnosticPhase("cli.config-snapshot", async () => {
  await withDiagnosticPhase("server.import-start", async () => {
    await innerPending;
  });
});

setTimeout(() => {
  resolveInner?.();
  outer.then(() => {
    process.stderr.write = origWrite;
    process.stdout.write("[smoke] complete\n");
    process.exit(0);
  });
}, 3000);
EOF

node_modules/.bin/tsx smoke-watchdog.ts
```

The smoke harness arms the real watchdog with a 1500 ms threshold, builds a real two-deep `withDiagnosticPhase` stack (outer `cli.config-snapshot` wraps inner `server.import-start`), and refuses to resolve the inner promise so the inner phase stays in-flight when the watchdog fires. The stderr-capture shim only re-routes the byte stream so the line is visible in the smoke transcript; the watchdog itself still writes via the real `process.stderr.write` it would use in production.

**Evidence after fix:** Literal terminal stderr output captured from the live run above against this PR's HEAD `dc1972766a` (single line as emitted by `process.stderr.write` in `src/logging/gateway-startup-watchdog.ts`):

```
STDERR-CAPTURE> [startup-watchdog] stuck step="server.import-start" elapsed=1501.0ms threshold=1500ms pending=["cli.config-snapshot"@1501.1ms "server.import-start"@1501.0ms]
[smoke] complete
```

The `STDERR-CAPTURE> ` prefix is added by the capture shim so the source channel (`stderr` vs `stdout`) is unambiguous in the transcript; the bytes after the prefix are the literal payload the watchdog wrote. Note that the `pending=[...]` entries now wrap each phase name in JSON quotes (`"cli.config-snapshot"@…ms` rather than `cli.config-snapshot@…ms`) — that is the Phase-2 escape discipline now applied to BOTH the `stuck step=` field and the `pending=[...]` render so an embedded `"` or `\n` in a future phase name cannot break the single-line parse contract.

**Observed result after fix:** End-to-end runtime behavior observed from the live stderr capture above, against the real `withDiagnosticPhase` + `armStartupWatchdog` modules shipped in this PR:

- A single newline-terminated line was emitted to `process.stderr` once the 1500 ms `setTimeout` callback ran. No second line ever followed, confirming the once-only fire semantics in the live runtime.
- The `stuck step="server.import-start"` token correctly identified the deepest currently-in-flight `withDiagnosticPhase` block as the wedged phase, taken from the real `activePhaseStack` at the moment the timer callback executed.
- The `pending=["cli.config-snapshot"@1501.1ms "server.import-start"@1501.0ms]` tail rendered the full live ancestor chain outermost → innermost with each phase's real wall-clock elapsed time computed from `performance.now()` at snapshot time.
- The elapsed times read 1501.0 ms / 1501.1 ms against the 1500 ms threshold — roughly 1 ms of real-world scheduling jitter, consistent with a healthy Node `setTimeout` on macOS.
- Both the `stuck step=` field AND the `pending=[...]` entries are JSON-quoted, so a future phase name containing a `"` or `\n` character cannot break the single-line parse contract operators will rely on when grepping production stderr. Covered by an explicit vitest case (`escapes embedded newlines and quotes in phase names so the line stays single-line`).
- Direct `process.stderr.write` carried the diagnostic out of the process without routing through `gatewayLog.*`, so a starved or filtered logger pipeline in the real hang case cannot eat the diagnostic — operationally exactly what point 3 of the design spec calls for.

**What was not tested:** A live `openclaw gateway` startup with a real Kubernetes pod and a real induced phase hang under `kubectl logs -f` was not exercised here — the smoke harness above runs the watchdog code path end-to-end in process but does not boot the full gateway. A follow-up real-cluster reproduction (deliberately stall one phase in `server.impl.ts:386` then watch `kubectl logs` for the stderr line) is a sensible next step before this is relied on for production triage, but the runtime behavior of the watchdog itself — timer firing, snapshot accuracy, single-line emit, format correctness — is covered above against the production modules.


---

## ⚠️ Known limitation — post-bind window is intentionally NOT covered

This watchdog disarms at **`http.bound`** (the `startupTrace.mark("http.bound")` point, after **all** bind hosts complete — cancel is fired there in `server.impl.ts`, deliberately *not* in the per-host `onListening` callback in `http-listen.ts`, so a multi-bind hang where the first host binds but `::1` stalls cannot silently disarm the watchdog between binds).

Consequently the watchdog covers the window **`[gateway] starting...` → HTTP `http.bound`** only. **Any hang that occurs *after* the socket has bound — most notably the post-bind plugin/sidecar load path (`loadGatewayStartupPlugins`, the original GH-5814 wedge class) — is invisible to this watchdog by design.** Once `http.bound` fires, the timer is cancelled; a subsequent wedge produces no `[startup-watchdog]` line.

This is an accepted scope boundary for this PR (diagnostic coverage of the *pre-bind* startup phases, which today emit nothing on hang). Extending coverage into the post-bind sidecar-load window is deliberately out of scope here and would require a separate disarm seam (e.g. arming a second watchdog across plugin load, disarmed at first-ready) — tracked separately, not in this change.

## Rebase note (source-line anchors)

This branch has been rebased onto current `upstream/main` (`418d7e1e83`); the `a1f1895b1b`-based "Source-line navigation" table above is therefore **approximate** — absolute line numbers have shifted. On the rebased tree the canonical anchors are: arm at `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts:832` (outside the per-respawn `start:` closure, which opens at `:842`, so it arms once per gateway-loop entry, not per respawn); primary disarm at the `http.bound` mark in `src/gateway/server.impl.ts`; secondary disarm in the `run.ts` startup-failure `catch`. The diff itself is the source of truth for exact placement.

target.title: Expose paged channel action results
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve the existing Discord `thread-list` raw `threads` result while adding normalized pagination metadata at the action boundary.
- Surface `complete`, `hasMore`, `returnedCount`, `source`, `query`, and `nextBefore` for archived thread pages when Discord reports `has_more`.
- Cover the archived-thread `has_more` regression and the missing archive timestamp fallback in the Discord action runtime tests.

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/actions/runtime.test.ts`
- `PATH=/Users/jason/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/simple-completion-runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --codex-bin /Users/jason/.codex/openclaw-pr-autopilot/bin/codex-persist-sessions --model codex=gpt-5.5 --thinking codex=high`
- Crabbox/Testbox E2E on the earlier PR head: provider `<test-provider>`, lease `<test-lease>`, Actions run `<actions-run-url>`; checked out `<pr-ref>` and ran `corepack pnpm test:docker:npm-onboard-discord-channel-agent`, result `npm tarball onboard/channel/agent Docker E2E passed (discord)`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord thread-list actions no longer hide Discord pagination under the raw provider payload; agents now get explicit completion and cursor metadata while existing callers can still read the prior raw `threads` field.

Real environment tested: A locally configured Discord bot in a test guild `<guild-id>`, using read-only OpenClaw message actions with the local bundled Discord plugin override so the patched source plugin was exercised.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=extensions OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 pnpm openclaw message thread list --channel discord --guild-id <guild-id> --channel-id <channel-id> --include-archived --limit 2 --json`

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the local Discord `<channel-name>` archived-thread command:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "items": [],
  "complete": true,
  "hasMore": false,
  "returnedCount": 0,
  "source": "discord.threadList.archived",
  "query": {
    "guildId": "<guild-id>",
    "channelId": "<channel-id>",
    "includeArchived": true,
    "limit": 2
  },
  "threads": {
    "threads": [],
    "members": [],
    "has_more": false
  },
  "members": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: The active guild thread-list and archived `<channel-name>` thread-list both completed successfully through the Discord plugin and produced the normalized contract shape. A read-only sweep of local text channels found no archived threads, so the live result proves the compatible empty-page path without creating or uploading any Discord content.

What was not tested: A live non-empty archived-thread page with `has_more: true`, because the local Discord server had no archived threads in the swept text channels and I did not create or post test data. The paged cursor path is covered by focused regression coverage in this PR.

target.title: fix(feishu): add searchable streaming fallback
target.body:
## Summary
- add an opt-in `channels.feishu.streamingSearchFallback` setting
- send the final streamed Feishu card text as a plain text fallback when enabled so Feishu conversation search can index it
- document the Feishu search limitation and visible duplicate-message tradeoff

Fixes #74767

## Tests
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/tools-config.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/policy.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/accounts.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts docs/channels/feishu.md extensions/feishu/src/config-schema.ts extensions/feishu/openclaw.plugin.json`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu users searching their conversation history for content from streamed assistant replies could not find any matches (#74767), because OpenClaw's Feishu streaming path closes the final text into a CardKit interactive message (`msg_type: "interactive"`), and Feishu's conversation-search index does not cover interactive card content; only `text` and `post` messages are indexed, per https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/im-v1/message/create.

Real environment tested: Linux, real OpenClaw checkout at this PR head, Node 22.19. The repro drives the actual production `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` (the function this PR changes) through a complete streamed final turn with `channels.feishu.streamingSearchFallback` first false, then true. The streaming card session, `send.ts`, the Lark client request construction, and the dispatcher gating are all unmodified production code; the only thing replaced is the literal network socket: the Lark SDK HTTP transport and the SSRF-guarded fetch the streaming card uses are swapped for an in-memory capturer that records every outbound Feishu API request and returns canned success bodies. No live Feishu credentials are exercised.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/repro-74808-proof.test.ts
```
The repro registers the in-memory capturer, builds a config for each setting value, drives one full streamed final reply through `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` (deliver final, then idle/close), and records every outbound Feishu API request with its `msg_type`.

Evidence after fix: copied live output (full before/after capture, no redaction needed) from this PR head:

```
final reply text:
Here is what I found in the deployment runbook:

- ssh into the bastion via `ssh deploy@bastion.prod.example.com`
- run `./scripts/rollout.sh canary --region=us-east-1`
- watch `./scripts/health-check.sh --watch` until p99 latency returns to baseline

Cite this in #incident-2026-05-15.

=== BEFORE  streamingSearchFallback: false (current main behavior) ===
  [cardkit fetch] POST /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards
  [lark-sdk im.message] POST /open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_parent/reply msg_type="interactive"
  [cardkit fetch] PUT /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_1/elements/content/content
  [cardkit fetch] PUT /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_1/elements/note/content
  [cardkit fetch] PATCH /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_1/settings
  -> message sends: interactive
  -> search-indexable send present (msg_type text/post): NO

=== AFTER   streamingSearchFallback: true (this PR) ===
  [cardkit fetch] POST /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards
  [lark-sdk im.message] POST /open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_parent/reply msg_type="interactive"
  [cardkit fetch] PUT /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_2/elements/content/content
  [cardkit fetch] PUT /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_2/elements/note/content
  [cardkit fetch] PATCH /open-apis/cardkit/v1/cards/ckcrd_fake_2/settings
  [lark-sdk im.message] POST /open-apis/im/v1/messages/om_parent/reply msg_type="post"
  -> message sends: interactive, post
  -> search-indexable send present (msg_type text/post): YES
  -> searchable post payload content: {"zh_cn":{"content":[[{"tag":"md","text":"Here is what I found in the deployment runbook:\n\n- ssh into the bastion via `ssh deploy@bastion.prod.example.com`\n- run `./scripts/rollout.sh canary --region=us-east-1`\n- watch `./scripts/health-check.sh --watch` until p99 latency returns to baseline\n\nCite this in #incident-2026-05-15."}]]}}
```

Observed result after fix: with the flag off (current main), the streamed final turn produces only the interactive streaming card (`msg_type: "interactive"`), so no search-indexable message is emitted; this is the symptom in #74767. With `channels.feishu.streamingSearchFallback: true`, the dispatcher additionally invokes `sendSearchableStreamingFallback` after the card closes, and the captured outbound request is an `im.message.reply` with `msg_type: "post"` whose content carries the full final reply text verbatim, which Feishu's conversation-search index covers. The duplicate visible message is the documented tradeoff and the setting stays default-off, so existing Feishu deployments are unaffected.

What was not tested: a live Feishu Open Platform round-trip against a real tenant, which requires Feishu app credentials and a configured chat. The capture above is the actual outbound JSON the production dispatcher produces; whether Feishu's search index ultimately surfaces the post is governed by Feishu's documented `msg_type` indexing rules cited above. A maintainer with a Feishu test tenant can confirm the end-to-end search hit if desired.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): complete catalog, onboarding, and tiered pricing
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #91238.

This PR completes the OpenCode Go catalog repair initially attempted in #91351. The prior fix was incomplete — it only added Flat Pricing assertions in test files without implementing the official tiered pricing in `provider-catalog.ts`.

### Changes

- **New model entries** in `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:
  - `minimax-m3`: `anthropic-messages` API, **512K context** (per OpenCode Go), $0.90/$3.60 (3× base pricing — see note below)
  - `qwen3.7-max`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $2.50/$7.50
  - `qwen3.7-plus`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $0.40/$1.60 (≤256K tier)
  - `hy3-preview`, `mimo-v2-omni`, `mimo-v2-pro` — additional bundled entries
- **API protocol fix**: `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from `openai-completions` to `anthropic-messages` (matching upstream)
- **Tiered pricing**: `qwen3.7-plus` and `qwen3.6-plus` now use proper split pricing at 256K tokens per [OpenCode Go docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/)
- **Context fix**: `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from 262K → 1M context window
- **Pricing fix**: `minimax-m2.5` and `minimax-m2.7` `cacheWrite` corrected from $0 → proper values
- **Onboarding fix**: `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` now actually applies the bundled catalog (previously returned `cfg` unchanged)
- **Tests and docs** updated to match the runtime catalog

### ⚠️ Pricing note: minimax-m3 (3× usage multiplier)

The [OpenCode Go documentation](https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/) lists MiniMax M3 base pricing at $0.30/$1.20 per 1M tokens. However, the OpenCode Go app applies a **3× usage multiplier** to MiniMax M3, making the effective per-token consumption in the subscription:

| Component | Base (docs) | ×3 effective |
|-----------|-------------|-------------|
| Input     | $0.30       | **$0.90**   |
| Output    | $1.20       | **$3.60**   |
| Cache Read| $0.06       | **$0.18**   |
| Cache Write| —          | N/A         |

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: OpenCode Go bundled catalog completeness and tiered pricing accuracy. The three models (`qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `minimax-m3`) were returning `model_not_found` when referenced in config (see #91238 logs).
- **Environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS with the bundled runtime.
- **Steps after patch**: run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts` to verify catalog entries resolve correctly with proper contexts, pricing, and API format.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  - `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max` resolves with `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $2.50/$7.50 pricing
  - `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus` resolves with `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, tiered pricing (≤256K: $0.40/$1.60, >256K: $1.20/$4.80)
  - `opencode-go/minimax-m3` resolves with `anthropic-messages` API, 512K context, $0.90/$3.60 (3× multiplier), multi-modal input
  - `opencode-go/qwen3.6-plus` API corrected to `anthropic-messages`, context corrected to 1M, tiered pricing applied
  - Onboarding now wires the bundled catalog into config at plugin init time
- **What was not tested**: live billing from the upstream service.

### Prior art
- #91351 — initial (incomplete) attempt, test stubs only
- #90088 — similar `model_not_found` issue resolved for Anthropic Haiku

target.title: fix(agents): classify structured unsupported model errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve structured provider error details for failover classification without changing stored/displayed `errorMessage`.
- Classify OpenAI-compatible `Not supported model <model-id>` details as `model_not_found` instead of schema/tool-payload failures.
- Add typed assistant error metadata and full-path regression coverage for thrown SDK-shaped errors, assistant `errorBody`, user-facing payloads, and OpenAI-compatible transport loopback.

Closes #92118

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI-compatible providers can return HTTP 400 bodies where `error.message` is `Param Incorrect` while `error.param` contains `Not supported model <model-id>`. OpenClaw now classifies that as `model_not_found` and avoids misleading schema/tool-payload copy.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_f55aeccc0a97` (`c7a.8xlarge`), run `run_de58983b891d`, using the dirty branch sync with a loopback OpenAI-compatible HTTP server returning the exact reported payload.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Crabbox script started a local `/v1/chat/completions` loopback server returning `{"error":{"code":"400","message":"Param Incorrect","param":"Not supported model some-model-id"}}`, then called `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn()` and checked `resolveFailoverReasonFromError()`, `classifyAssistantFailoverReason()`, and `formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText()`.

Evidence after fix: The Crabbox proof printed `OPENCLAW_92118_FIXED_PROOF` with `dependencyShapeClassification.reason: "model_not_found"`, `classifications.assistantFailoverReason: "model_not_found"`, and user-facing text `The selected model was not found by the provider. Check the model id or choose a different model.`

Observed result after fix: The exact reported payload no longer falls through to schema/tool-payload copy.

What was not tested: A live third-party Xiaomi/MiFY credentialed provider call was not run; the proof uses a local OpenAI-compatible loopback server with the exact reported response body and real OpenClaw OpenAI-compatible transport.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (ran twice after formatting; final result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/live-model-errors.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (2 shards, 616 tests passed)
- `../crabbox/bin/crabbox run --provider aws ...` (`run_de58983b891d`, `cbx_f55aeccc0a97`) for the reported loopback repro/fix proof
- `git diff --check`

target.title: Fail closed for CLI-backed /btw fallback
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92168.

- fail closed when `/btw` would otherwise fall back to direct provider auth for a model routed through a CLI runtime alias
- preserve existing `/btw` direct fallback behavior for non-CLI/custom harnesses without side-question hooks
- add regression coverage proving CLI-runtime alias models do not call direct provider auth or direct streaming

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "fails closed instead of using direct provider auth for CLI-runtime alias models|keeps the direct provider fallback for non-Codex harnesses without side-question hooks"`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD -- src/agents/btw.ts src/agents/btw.test.ts`
- Crabbox real behavior proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_f4e7388d8cd9`, run `run_86ad8a73c5b5`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/btw` no longer silently switches a CLI-runtime-backed model to direct provider auth/streaming when no side-question hook is available.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Node/Vitest agent test lane; AWS Crabbox Linux run from the production `/btw` command handler with isolated temp session state and no provider credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the full and focused Vitest commands listed above; Crabbox script invoked `handleBtwCommand` with a real persisted session transcript and `agents.defaults.models["anthropic/claude-opus-4-7"].agentRuntime.id = "claude-cli"`.

Evidence after fix: full `src/agents/btw.test.ts` passed 34/34; focused regression proof passed 2/2; Crabbox proof printed `OPENCLAW_92168_PROOF_PASS` and exited 0.

Observed result after fix: CLI-runtime alias `/btw` returns `⚠️ /btw failed: /btw is not yet supported for claude-cli-backed models... OpenClaw will not fall back to direct anthropic auth...`; the preserved non-CLI custom harness fallback reaches direct Anthropic auth lookup instead of being blocked as a CLI alias.

target.title: Fix OTLP log trace correlation
target.body:
## Summary

- fix OTLP log correlation for OpenClaw-owned logger records emitted inside active diagnostic trace scopes
- keep explicit logger-supplied trace objects untrusted so plugin/user-provided trace IDs cannot spoof OTLP ancestry
- extend the OTEL QA smoke to fail unless exported log records include trace/span correlation IDs while default log bodies remain placeholders

Fixes #91865 for the trace/span correlation portion. This intentionally does not change default OTLP log body export; log bodies remain gated by content capture.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` initially found one actionable test assertion issue; fixed it and reran clean: `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/logging/diagnostic-log-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/qa-otel-smoke.test.ts`
- Crabbox/Testbox live E2E: `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf`, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, command `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`, exit `0`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OTLP log records emitted from OpenClaw-owned active diagnostic trace scopes now carry OTLP trace/span correlation context while preserving placeholder log bodies by default.
Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux with a real `otel/opentelemetry-collector:0.104.0` container and mock OpenAI QA provider.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `corepack pnpm qa:otel:collector-smoke --output-dir .artifacts/qa-e2e/issue-91865-fixed-testbox`
Evidence after fix: Testbox `tbx_01ktwd5egrdrx7fa924hzj7hnf` passed the updated smoke, which decodes OTLP log `traceId`/`spanId` fields and fails if no log record includes both IDs.
Observed result after fix: `qa-otel-smoke: passed spans=17 metrics=17 logs=9 traces=3 metricRequests=3 logRequests=3`
What was not tested: A third-party production OTLP backend UI; the proof validates the OTLP payload through a real collector and decoded receiver.

target.title: Fix configured DeepSeek model transport inheritance
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92148.

Configured provider model rows that only override metadata now inherit bundled static catalog transport for known bundled provider models instead of falling through to the OpenAI Responses default. This keeps configured/runtime transport overrides ahead of static fallback metadata, treats blank configured `baseUrl` values as absent, and makes the built manifest scanner use the shared bundled plugin directory resolver so packaged runtime can find bundled static metadata.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-metadata-scan.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.static-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Crabbox provider `azure`, lease `cbx_3afa72390208`, run `run_152bba89a0c0`:
  - focused tests passed remotely
  - patched remote build passed
  - patched live packaged DeepSeek probe returned `status: ok` for `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`
  - reversed-patch regression baseline rebuilt and reproduced the old OpenAI-routed 401 path

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: configured DeepSeek model rows without explicit transport no longer route to the OpenAI Responses API.

Real environment tested: Azure Crabbox Linux lease `cbx_3afa72390208`, run `run_152bba89a0c0`, with `DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` injected from 1Password only into the proof command.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: built the packaged CLI on Crabbox, then ran `node ./openclaw.mjs models status --json --probe --probe-provider deepseek --probe-timeout 45000 --probe-concurrency 1 --probe-max-tokens 8` against an isolated config whose default model was `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` and whose configured DeepSeek model row omitted `api` and `baseUrl`.

Evidence after fix: the patched live probe summary was `status: ok`, `provider: deepseek`, `model: deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`, with no `openai-responses`, OpenAI API-key URL, or 401 marker.

Observed result after fix: OpenClaw used the DeepSeek-compatible chat transport inherited from bundled static metadata and authenticated successfully against DeepSeek.

What was not tested: no GUI flow; this issue is in model/provider transport resolution and was verified through the packaged CLI probe path.

target.title: Fix suppressed heartbeat commitment delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91948.

- Treat durable `suppressed` heartbeat sends as non-visible delivery, not successful commitment delivery.
- Keep due commitments pending and avoid writing heartbeat dedupe state when the durable send is suppressed.
- Emit a skipped heartbeat event with the durable suppression reason, then allow a later visible heartbeat to retry and mark the commitment sent.
- Add a regression test covering a suppressed `No channel reply.` heartbeat followed by a visible retry.

## Root Cause

`runHeartbeatOnce` called `sendDurableMessageBatch`, threw only for failed/partial failed statuses, and then treated every other result as delivered. That meant a `suppressed` durable result from outbound planning could mark inferred commitments `sent`, write `lastHeartbeatText`/`lastHeartbeatSentAt`, and emit a `sent` heartbeat event even though no channel message was visible.

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean; no accepted/actionable findings reported; overall patch correct `(0.86)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.commitments.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - 10 tests passed after rebase.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/route-reply.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` - 54 tests passed.
- Regression repro: with only the runtime fix temporarily reverted, the new focused test failed on current behavior with `expected 'sent' to deeply equal 'pending'`; after restoring the fix, the focused test passed.
- Live AWS Crabbox proof, run `run_6ac6aacdd5f2`: first suppressed Telegram heartbeat left the commitment `pending`, kept `sentAtMs` unset, did not write heartbeat dedupe state, and emitted `skipped` with `reason: no_visible_payload`; the next visible heartbeat sent exactly one Telegram message and marked the commitment `sent`.

commands.author: zenglingbiao

target.title: feat(cli): add --file option to image generate command (fixes #91734)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw infer image generate` has no `--file` option, while `openclaw infer image edit` does. The shared runner `runImageGenerate` already accepts `file?: string[]` and forwards them as `inputImages`, so this is a pure CLI parity gap.
- **Root Cause**: The `image.generate` command registration in `src/cli/capability-cli.ts` omits `--file` and its action does not forward files to `runImageGenerate`.
- **Fix**: Add `--file <path>` option (repeatable, via `collectOption`) to `image.generate`, parse the collected values, and pass them as `file` to `runImageGenerate` — mirroring the existing `image.edit` pattern.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/cli/capability-cli.ts` — metadata: add `"--file"` to image.generate flags; command: add `.option("--file <path>", "Input file", collectOption, [])` and forward files to `runImageGenerate`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - Provider-level "unsupported input images" handling — already exists in the provider path
  - Image output, format, background, or other options — unchanged

### Reproduction

1. `openclaw infer image generate --help` — observe no `--file` option
2. `openclaw infer image edit --help` — observe `--file` is available
3. **Before this PR**: `openclaw infer image generate --prompt "branded cover" --file logo.png` fails with "unknown option"
4. **After this PR**: `--file` is accepted, files are read and forwarded as `inputImages` to the generation provider

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#91734)**: The `image generate` CLI command now accepts `--file <path>` (repeatable), reads the input files, and forwards them as `inputImages` to the image generation runtime — matching the existing `image edit` parity.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — CI-equivalent test harness exercising the full CLI path through `runRegisteredCli` with a temporary PNG file.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  96 passed (96)
   Start at  20:32:22
   Duration  4.96s (transform 1.89s, setup 317ms, import 3.26s, tests 1.18s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.71s
```

**Observed result after fix**: The new test "forwards --file from image generate to runImageGenerate" creates a temporary PNG file, invokes `image generate --file <path>` through the full CLI, and verifies the resolved `inputImages` array reaches `generateImage` with the correct filename — proving end-to-end forwarding. The existing 95 tests continue to produce the expected results with no regressions.

**What was not tested**: Live provider round-trip with actual image generation was not driven; the existing provider path for unsupported input-images was not re-tested.

**Repro confirmation**: Before this PR, `image generate --help` does not list `--file`, and passing `--file` is rejected. After this PR, the test confirms `--file` is parsed, the file is read, and the resolved `inputImages` are forwarded to `generateImage`.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: Providers that don't support input images for generation may receive `inputImages` unexpectedly.
  **Mitigation**: The existing provider path (e.g. `openai-compatible-image-provider.ts`) already checks for non-empty `inputImages` and fails explicitly when unsupported. No new failure mode is introduced.
- **Risk**: Silent fallback to text-only generation when files are supplied.
  **Mitigation**: Existing provider implementations treat `inputImages` as edit/reference mode; they do not silently ignore them.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix (CLI parity gap)

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI
- [x] image-generation

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/capability-cli.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #91734

target.title: Fix disabled heartbeat one-shot cron retries
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91775.

- add an exact removable system-event enqueue handle for cron-delivered main-session system events
- retry disabled `wakeMode: "now"` one-shot main-session cron jobs with the existing bounded cron retry/backoff policy instead of disabling them immediately
- remove failed-attempt queued system events so retries do not leave orphaned prompt events, while preserving completion only for a heartbeat that actually runs and consumes the event
- include restart catch-up coverage for persisted skipped/disabled one-shot retry state

## Verification

- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
  - first pass found a stale `src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts` mock for the new system-events export
  - fixed the mock and re-ran clean with no accepted/actionable findings
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/service.runs-one-shot-main-job-disables-it.test.ts src/cron/service.restart-catchup.test.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/infra/system-events.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Crabbox AWS live repro/proof:
  - provider: `aws`
  - run: `run_1c0ed22cf056`
  - lease: `cbx_883e31de8e01`
  - slug: `pearl-shrimp`
  - type: `c7a.8xlarge`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: disabled heartbeat no longer makes an immediate main-session one-shot cron job look terminal while leaving its queued system event behind.

Real environment tested: Crabbox Linux on AWS, provider `aws`, run `run_1c0ed22cf056`, lease `cbx_883e31de8e01`, slug `pearl-shrimp`, type `c7a.8xlarge`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the Crabbox script installed Node `v22.19.0` and pnpm `11.2.2`, created a detached `origin/main` worktree at `575cae59d4`, symlinked dependencies, then ran the same cron proof harness against `origin/main` and this branch with `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-91775-proof.mjs`.

Evidence after fix: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for the fixed branch reported `heartbeatCalls: 3`, `consumedTexts: ["hello"]`, `finalQueued: []`, two disabled retry snapshots with no queued events, and a final successful attempt where the one-shot no longer existed.

Observed result after fix: the first two disabled heartbeat attempts kept the one-shot enabled for bounded retry and removed each failed-attempt queued event; the third attempt consumed exactly one `"hello"` system event and deleted the completed one-shot.

What was not tested: a real Telegram/Discord channel round-trip was not needed for this fix because the bug and fix are in cron heartbeat state transition and in-memory system-event queue ownership; the live proof exercised the gateway-facing queue contract and real cron service path.

## Current-main regression proof

The same Crabbox run reproduced current `origin/main` behavior: `PROOF_SUMMARY` for base reported `enqueueReturnsRemovableHandle: false`, `heartbeatCalls: 1`, the job still existed with `enabled: false`, `lastStatus: "skipped"`, `lastError: "disabled"`, and `finalQueued` contained the queued `"hello"` event.
